title: Islanders Win in Overtime
snippets: The New York Islanders forced overtime with an extra attacker in the final two minutes, then won it in the extra session 4-3. Brian Wilde has more.
Matthew Schaefer had a pair of goals, Ilya Sorokin made 21 saves and J.G. Pageau buried the overtime winning goal to lift the New York Islanders over the Montreal Canadiens 4-3.
Marchand scores two as Panthers down Maple Leafs 5-1 The Globe and Mail
Sergei Bobrovsky made 28 saves on the night, Brad Marchand added two goals to his season tally, and the Florida Panthers cruised to a 5-1 win over the Toronto Maple Leafs.

Jake Walman scored twice, Leon Draisaitl piled up a goal and three assists, Zach Hyman added a goal and two helpers, and Connor McDavid hit the 100-point mark as the Oilers lit up both goalies in an 8-1 win over the Los Angeles Kings.
John Gibson made 26 saves, while Dylan Larkin found the back of the net twice including the overtime winner to help the Detroit Red Wings beat the Ottawa Senators 2-1.
Nazem Kadri scored twice, with Connor Zary and Mikael Backlund also chipping in, and Dustin Wolf made 34 saves in the Calgary Flames’ 4-1 win over the San Jose Sharks.
Samuel Ersson made 23 saves, Trevor Zegras scored and Matvei Michkov buried twice including the overtime winner to help the Philadelphia Flyers beat the New York Rangers 3-2.
Viktor Arvidsson scored a pair of goals, Joonas Korpisalo stopped a whopping 36 shots on the night, and the Boston Bruins took a 4-2 win over the Columbus Blue Jackets.
Connor Clifton scored the game-winning goal in the third period, and Arturs Silovs stopped 28 of 29 shots as the Pittsburgh Penguins defeated the New Jersey Devils 4–1.
Nikolaj Ehlers opened the scoring early in the first, and Sebastian Aho scored the game-winner in the third period as the Carolina Hurricanes defeated the Tampa Bay Lightning 5–4.
Matthew Wood tied the game in the third period, and Ryan O’Reilly scored the game-winner late, as the Nashville Predators defeated the Chicago Blackhawks 4-2.

Dylan Holloway netted his first NHL hat trick and added an assist in his return from injury, while Joel Hoffer made 23 stops as the St. Louis Blues snapped a three-game skid with a 5-1 win over the Seattle Kraken.
titles: Call of the Wilde: New York Islanders rebound to beat Montreal Canadiens 4-3 in OT
